answer:
We can test consistency by trying to assign truth values to make all statements true.  
From N · ~Q, N is true and Q is false.  
Then ~N ≡ (O · P) becomes false ≡ (O · P), so O · P must be false.  
~O ⊃ Q becomes ~O ⊃ false, so ~O must be false, meaning O is true.  
With O true, O · P false forces P false. This matches option D.  

\boxed{D}
